title: Atlas Villages Walk from Imlil
short_description: Guided valley walk with tea in a Berber home and views of Toubkal.
long_description: Leave Marrakech early for the High Atlas. Your guide leads a moderate half-day loop through apple orchards and stone villages above Imlil, with breaks for mint tea and bread baked in a family kitchen. Suitable for most fitness levels; poles available on request.

title: Fès Medina Masterclass: Tanneries & Brass
short_description: Navigate the medina with a certified guide; leather souq and Nejjarine square.
long_description: Step into Fès el-Bali with a guide who knows every dead end. Visit the Chouara tanneries from above, learn how hides become babouches, then wind to the brassworkers and spice stalls. Includes a stop for harira and msemen in a tucked-away fondouk.

title: Chefchaouen Blue Lanes & Rif Views
short_description: Morning photo walk, kasbah visit, and café stop overlooking the blue alleys.
long_description: Meet before the day-trippers arrive. Your local host shows the best indigo corners, the old kasbah, and a short climb for a panorama over the Rif. Finish with fresh orange juice in Outa el Hammam square.

title: Erg Chebbi Sunset Camel & Berber Camp
short_description: Ride into the dunes at golden hour; dinner, drums, and stars at a private camp.
long_description: Transfer from Merzouga village to the dune line. Camel trek of about an hour reaches a low-impact camp with proper tents, hot shower block, and a three-course dinner. Astronomer-friendly skies; early optional climb for sunrise.

title: Essaouira Ramparts & Port Lunch
short_description: Sea breeze, Skala du Port, and grilled catch steps from the boats.
long_description: Walk the 18th-century sea walls, visit the lively fish auction, and sit down for a simple lunch of the day’s catch with salad and bread. Afternoon free for galleries or the beach; return transfer from Marrakech available as add-on.

title: Jemaa el-Fna After Dark Food Circuit
short_description: Small-group tasting: snail soup, skewers, msemen, and secret rooftop tea.
long_description: When the square fills with smoke and drums, a food-savvy guide leads you past tourist traps to stalls locals still use. Vegetarian swaps available. Ends with nus-nus coffee or tea above the chaos.

title: Riad Kitchen: Fassi Pastilla Workshop
short_description: Hands-on class: warqa, spiced pigeon or chicken filling, icing sugar finish.
long_description: In a restored riad kitchen, learn how Fès makes its famous layered pie. Market shopping optional add-on. You sit down to eat what you bake, with wine or mocktails and printed recipes.

title: Merzouga Stargazing & Desert Stories
short_description: Late session away from camp lights: constellations, Berber sky lore, hot drinks.
long_description: Short 4x4 hop to a dark-sand ridge. Guide points out seasonal constellations and shares oral histories tied to the Sahara. Binoculars provided; blankets and tea included. Can pair with overnight camp package.

title: Sidecar Spin: Palmeraie & Guéliz
short_description: Vintage sidecar ride through palm groves and art-deco corners.
long_description: One guest rides pillion, one in the tub; swap halfway. Helmet, jacket, and insurance included. Stops for photos and a short mint tea in the palmeraie.

title: Ben Youssef & Secret Courtyards
short_description: Skip-the-line Ben Youssef visit plus doors-only locals know.
long_description: Morning slot at the restored madrasa, then narrow lanes to private riad courtyards opened for small groups. Respectful dress encouraged; photography allowed where signed.

title: Fès Pottery Wheel Session
short_description: Throw a tagine lid on a kick wheel in a family atelier.
long_description: Short demo, then hands-on shaping; pieces are fired later and can ship internationally for a fee. Aprons and clay provided.

title: Chefchaouen Waterfall Hike (Akchour)
short_description: Half-day outing to the God’s Bridge trail with picnic.
long_description: Scenic drive from Chefchaouen, then a guided moderate hike along the river. Swim optional in summer; sturdy shoes required.

title: Essaouira Surf Try-Out
short_description: Beach lesson with ISA-style safety briefing and soft boards.
long_description: Two hours in small waves at a sheltered spot. Wetsuits in cooler months. Showers nearby; changing rooms basic.

title: Merzouga Fat-Bike Dunes
short_description: Electric fat-bikes on hard-packed lanes between ergs.
long_description: Not for deep sand slogging—this loop uses jeep tracks and firmer edges with big views. Helmet, gloves, water. Sunset departure option.

title: Fès Calligraphy Mini-Lesson
short_description: Learn Maghrebi script with bamboo qalam and ink.
long_description: Hosted in a craft studio near the Andalus quarter. You keep your practice sheet; no Arabic required.

title: Marrakech Cycling: Agafay Edge
short_description: Gravel e-bike loop toward Agafay stone desert.
long_description: Support vehicle on call; flat to rolling terrain. Snack and water included. Not technical but sun exposure is real—bring SPF.

title: Essaouira Gnaoua Intro Concert (Private)
short_description: Intimate set with explanation of rhythms and ritual context.
long_description: Held in a small venue or riad courtyard. Respectful, non-exploitative booking; musicians paid fair session rates.

title: Chefchaouen Weaving Demo
short_description: Watch loom work and natural dyes; fair-trade shop optional.
long_description: Family-run atelier explains wool sourcing and indigo history. No hard sell; tipping guide appreciated.

title: Riad Dar Saffarine
short_description: Roof terrace over the Sebou gorge of roofs; family hospitality.
long_description: Eight rooms around a tiled courtyard, homemade breakfasts, and honest advice on which medina gates to use after dark. Airport transfers on request.

title: Kasbah-style Lodge at Dune Line
short_description: Pool, date palms, and direct access to Erg Chebbi arrangements.
long_description: Spacious rooms with AC, restaurant on site, and front desk that coordinates camel camps without upselling. Family rooms available.

title: Casa Perleta
short_description: Blue-door guesthouse with lemon trees and honest Wi‑Fi.
long_description: Steep lanes last 80m—porters available. Excellent breakfast; staff helps with Akchour taxis.

title: Riad de la Mer
short_description: Sea breeze rooms two minutes from the ramparts.
long_description: Rooftop with loungers; some rooms have partial ocean glimpse. Quiet hours after 11pm.

title: Boutique Stay Guéliz
short_description: Art deco lobby, rooftop pool, walk to MACAAL.
long_description: Soundproof windows, gym, and concierge for day trips to Imlil or Ourika. Parking limited—valet on request.

title: Dar Zellij Marrakech
short_description: Four-room dar with plunge pool and in-house hammam slot.
long_description: Cooking class add-ons; children welcome with prior notice. No stag parties.

title: Café Clock Fès
short_description: Camel burger or veggie mezze in a restored courtyard.
long_description: Cultural programming some evenings; reservations recommended Friday. Cash and cards.

title: Chez Rachid (Essaouira Fish)
short_description: Pick your fish from the ice, grilled with charmoula and salads.
long_description: Plastic chairs, sea air, and the best value in the port strip. Arrive before 1pm for variety.

title: Dar Hatim
short_description: Set-menu Fassi classics in a family home.
long_description: Advance booking only; specify dietary needs 24h ahead. Alcohol not served; bring your own if policy allows that week.

title: Salut Maroc!
short_description: Color-block dining room; modern Moroccan plates and cocktails.
long_description: Popular with visitors; book terrace at sunset. Vegetarian tasting menu available.

title: Chez Hicham (Merzouga)
short_description: Berber pizza, tajines, and huge salads after the dunes.
long_description: Family-run; portions generous. Serves alcohol-free drinks only.

title: Aladdin Chefchaouen
short_description: Terrace views over the blue cascade; tagines and vegan options.
long_description: Tourist-friendly but consistent quality; go early for terrace tables.

title: Imperial Cities Express
short_description: Fès, Meknes side trip, Rabat Atlantic evening—six nights, private driver.
long_description: Curated riads, skip-the-line monuments where possible, and one free afternoon in Fès. Luggage van; pace moderate. Meknes visit is half-day including Volubilis viewpoint photo stop.
subtitle: History & medinas

title: South & Sahara Loop
short_description: Marrakech → Aït Benhaddou → Dades → Merzouga → back via Ouarzazate.
long_description: Three nights south including one desert camp, kasbah walks, and a rose-valley stroll in season. Some long driving days; audiobooks recommended.
subtitle: Dunes & kasbahs

title: Atlantic Chill Week
short_description: Essaouira base with day trips to Sidi Kaouki beach and argan cooperative.
long_description: Seven nights in a medina riad; surf add-on available. Includes cooking class and wine-free pairing dinner with mocktails.
subtitle: Wind, waves, medina

title: Blue Pearl & North Rif
short_description: Chefchaouen deep dive plus Tetouan medina day.
long_description: Four nights in Chefchaouen, guided Akchour hike, and licensed guide in Tetouan UNESCO medina. Border formalities if combining Ceuta—ask before booking.
subtitle: Rif mountains

title: Atlas Pathways Co-op
short_description: Certified mountain and Marrakech medina guides; fair-wage collective.
long_description: Member-owned cooperative with UIAGM leads for winter ascents and family-friendly valley walks. Liability insurance on file.

title: Sahara Horizon Camps
short_description: Low-impact desert camps, Merzouga; kitchen and maintenance staff year-round.
long_description: Works with local camel cooperatives; waste carried out; solar showers. B2B rates for agencies on request.

title: Coastal Routes Transport
short_description: Licensed fleet Marrakech–Essaouira and city hops on the Atlantic coast.
long_description: Mercedes V-class and Sprinter options; child seats; English-speaking dispatch.

title: Women-Only Atlas & Fès Week
short_description: October departure: workshops, hikes, and medina time with female leads.
long_description: Max 14 guests; shared twin rooms or single supplement. Includes one hammam ritual in Fès and a pottery session.
subtitle: Hosted journey for women travellers

title: New Year Dunes & Drums
short_description: Ring in 2027 around a fireline in Erg Chebbi—limited spots.
long_description: Two nights camp, transit from Marrakech by private minibus convoy (extra fee). Gala dinner 31 Dec; alcohol policy communicated in pre-trip PDF.
subtitle: Marrakech round-trip option
